Fort Carson. He and four other persons from his unit, including his as | drove the short distance over to the a That day it was pleasant with clear blue skies. After they parked, they were walking west towards the entrance of the building. It was natural to look west because of the beautiful view of Cheyanne Mountain. MH oat that time, 9M and the four other soldiers with him all noticed a very strange object that appeared to be hovering just over Cheyanne mountain, in a low point or "saddle" in the mountain. | described the object as being matte white, or off-white in color and appearing nonmetallic. The object was oval shaped and in a horizontal position, with a curved indentation on the bottom. i described the object as being "beanshaped". The object was completely motionless and no sound could be heard coming from it. The object was covered in intersect i ng lines or ridges which formed an abstract polygon pattern which covered the entire object. - stated the object was about the size of 10% of the saddl e of the mountains, or about the size of a pool cue at arms-length. None of the five men had a phone on them They all simultaneously looked away momentarily to decide to have one of them go back to the vehicle to get a phone to take a video. When they loo

ked back the obj ect had vanished. They searched the western skyline but wer e not able find the object again. ■ They were all confused what they shou l d do next, They got together afterwards to di scuss what they had seen. They wonde red if they had seen a UAP, and went through the alternatives it could have been, such as a blimp or a weather balloon. Each of them independently drew what they remembered the object to look like, and then t hey compared their drawings afterwards. All of the drawings were consistent i n showing a horizontal, white, "bean-shaped" object with abstract lines crossing on it. - believed that made an officia l report to the army about their observation. - stated he was contacted by AARO in early 2024 about their observation.
